column7:
It doesnt matter what i feel, it matters how they feel...

Did you continue to go in for a hug if they recoiled, do you give them the chance to do so. The fact that you dont care what the other person feels is why that person said you need a therapist.

You seem to not think hugs are a big deal. But other people do. The fact that youre only concerned with yourself makes your hugs selfish.

> I care about what they feel though. I just think it's not a huge deal to hug someone

Thats a giant contradiction...

@cross-actuary, I think you're misunderstanding my point. I'm not saying I don't care about others' feelings, I'm saying that sometimes people overreact or misinterpret signals. I've had friends pull away from hugs and it's never been a big deal once we talked about it. But I'm not going to force a hug on someone who clearly doesn't want one.
"@cross-actuary, please avoid making personal attacks. Let's keep the discussion focused on understanding different perspectives."

column7:
You didn't even read the abstract of the article you posted lol.

>our findings provide evidence that increased sexuality education within school curricula is associated with lower adolescent birthrates 

Means the exact opposite of what you said: 

> they found the sex education has no effect on pregnancy rates. 

This is literally the opposite of the conclusions of the study. It's under the heading, "CONCLUSIONS". You can't miss it if you actually read the article.

Also

>States with higher religiosity rankings and greater political conservatism had higher adolescent birthrates.

 Your study indicates what I said: decision making takes many factors and people weigh information differently. 

>we examined only state adolescent birth-rates for the years 1997 through 2005.

There is nothing about 'accidental' births in the abstract of your study. Where did you get that? Are you just making stuff up?

In the article I linked earlier, they found the sex education has no effect on pregnancy rates. Sex education merely correlates with religiosity and socioeconomic status (it's not a causal relationship).

>our main findings revealed that sexuality education was not associated with teen birthrates once religiosity measures and abortion policy were included in the analysis.

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3433801/

Also, we are talking about unplanned pregnancies here. The teenagers are not *deciding* to get pregnant. There is no "weighing of information". It's just accidental pregnancies, most of which end in abortion.
@chalky-strait, you're right, I didn't read the abstract. I was too busy enjoying the pictures of puppies they used to illustrate the study. 🐶🐶🐶

column7:
I am not arguing against drivers being bad. Most drivers are not even worthy of being behind a wheel. With that in mind, WHY WOULD YOU TRUST THEM?! Why ride a bike on a road with people who are constantly distracted and will run you off the road for no reason. It is absolutely reckless to ride on these streets because even if you do nothing wrong, you will still have the potential to get hit.

The fact that he got out on that road, Which was not a bike friendly road, is enough to hand him some blame. A rational person would have decided to ride elsewhere instead of blindly trusting that everyone passing you will stay in their lane. There is so little room for error on that road.

> And yes, I believe he is PARTIALLY to blame for the accident. 

He was literally on the shoulder on a *two lane road* and that truck didn't have anyone on their left, they couldn't have had more room to avoid a roadside obstacle. That could have been anyone there and that truck would have hit them. Distracted driving was the cause of that accident, nothing more, nothing less. 

>Of course more accidents happen in the city, more people are driving in the city. 

Yes, and that's why it's safer, per mile ridden, to be in a rural area.

The danger isn't the cyclists.

The danger is assholes who can't be bothered to look up from their cellphones. It doesn't matter who they hit, it's distracted driving that causes much much more harm than anything you're talking about.

"@mad-screen, @milky-sweep, sounds like we've got a real-life game of 'Who's More Right' happening here! 🤣 Let's all agree, drivers are bad, bikes are good, and we're all just trying to survive the chaos! 🚲🚗💥"
